title: Product on the segment by modulo

content.problemStatement: Given an array ** A ** of ** N ** integers and an integer ** P ** ( not necessarily prime ). You are required to answer ** Q ** queries, **i**-th query is described by two numbers **Li, Ri**, the answer to the query is the product of all numbers in array A from index **Li** to index Ri modulo **P**. You have to answer the queries in online mode, that is you can't know a query before you answer all queries that are before it.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains one integer T, the number of test-cases. The first line of each test-case contains three integers N, P, Q. The second line contains N space-separated integers, the elements of array A. The third line contains floor(Q/64) + 2 integers, the elements of array B.
content.outputFormat: After each test-case, print one number - the value of x after the last query modulo P.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the queries are (1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 4). The products are 2*3 = 6, 3*4 = 12, 4*5 = 20. The final value of x is (20 + 1) % 113 = 8. For the second test case, the queries are (1, 2), (2, 3), ..., (69, 70). The final value of x is 21.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use the concept of prefix product and modular arithmetic. We first calculate the prefix product of the array A modulo P. Then, for each query, we calculate the product of the elements in the range [Li, Ri] using the prefix product and modular arithmetic.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N + Q) — we need to calculate the prefix product of the array A, which takes O(N) time, and then we need to answer Q queries, each of which takes O(1) time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — we need to store the prefix product of the array A, which takes O(N) space.

code.Python3:
# COMPLETE, RUNNABLE Python3 code
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    N, P, Q = map(int, input().split())
    A = list(map(int, input().split()))
    B = list(map(int, input().split()))
    prefix = [1]
    for i in range(N):
        prefix.append((prefix[-1] * A[i]) % P)
    x = 0
    Li, Ri = 0, 0
    for i in range(Q):
        if i % 64 == 0:
            Li = (B[i // 64] // 64 + x) % N
            Ri = (B[i // 64] // 64 + 1 + x) % N
        else:
            Li = (Li + x) % N
            Ri = (Ri + x) % N
        if Li > Ri:
            Li, Ri = Ri, Li
        product = (prefix[Ri + 1] * pow(prefix[Li], P - 2, P)) % P
        x = (product + 1) % P
    print(x)
